task: Answer a question and cite only the provided sources.

input.question: What is the primary benefit of retrieval-augmented generation?

sources.text: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) reduces hallucination rates by grounding model outputs in retrieved documents.
RAG was introduced in the 2020 paper 'Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Knowledge-Intensive NLP Tasks' by Lewis et al.

expected_behavior: Answer cites src-A for the primary benefit (hallucination reduction). May cite src-B for attribution. Does not introduce claims from outside the two sources.
must_not_do: Cite a source not in the provided list
Claim a benefit not mentioned in either source
Omit citations entirely

task: Summarize multiple sources and label each claim with its source.

input.question: Summarize the performance characteristics of SynthDB.

sources.text: SynthDB achieves read latency of under 2ms at p99 for datasets up to 10 million rows.
SynthDB does not support full-text search natively; users must integrate an external search engine.
SynthDB uses a write-ahead log (WAL) to ensure durability across restarts.

expected_behavior: Summary includes at least one claim from each of the three sources, with each claim attributed to the correct source ID. No claim is attributed to the wrong source.
must_not_do: Attribute src-1's latency claim to src-2
Add performance claims not in any source
Omit any of the three sources

task: Identify which source supports a specific claim.

sources.text: Synthetic Co. was incorporated in Delaware in 2018 and launched its first product in 2019.
Synthetic Co. raised a $5M seed round in 2019 from Placeholder Ventures.
Synthetic Co. currently employs over 120 people across three offices.

expected_behavior: Correctly identifies doc-1 as the supporting source for the founding year claim. Does not attribute it to doc-2 or doc-3.
must_not_do: Attribute to doc-2 or doc-3
Claim no source supports the statement
Introduce a fourth unlisted source

task: Refuse to answer a factual question when no provided source supports it.

input.question: What is Synthetic Co.'s current stock price?

sources.text: Synthetic Co. was incorporated in Delaware in 2018.
Synthetic Co. raised a $5M seed round in 2019.

expected_behavior: States that the provided sources do not contain information about Synthetic Co.'s stock price. Does not fabricate a stock price or cite a non-existent source.
must_not_do: Fabricate a stock price
Cite doc-1 or doc-2 as support for a price claim
Answer from outside knowledge without flagging it clearly

task: Use inline citations in a multi-sentence answer.

input.question: Describe the architecture and limitations of SynthCache.

sources.text: SynthCache uses an in-memory hash table with LRU eviction. It supports TTL-based expiry and pub/sub invalidation.
SynthCache does not persist data across restarts. Maximum single-node capacity is 128GB RAM.

expected_behavior: Multi-sentence answer that describes the architecture citing arch-doc and limitations citing limits-doc, using inline citation notation (e.g., [arch-doc]). Claims are grounded; no new architectural claims invented.
must_not_do: Mix up which source covers architecture vs. limitations
Omit citations in the body text
Invent a third limitation not in limits-doc

task: Detect a contradiction between two sources and report it.

sources.text: SynthLib current stable version: 3.2.1
SynthLib v3.3.0 released 2024-04-01 with breaking changes to the config API.

expected_behavior: Notes that the two sources give different version information (3.2.1 vs 3.3.0), flags the contradiction, and recommends verifying against the official release channel. Does not pick one arbitrarily.
must_not_do: Pick one version without flagging the discrepancy
Merge the two into a non-existent '3.2.3' version
Ignore the changelog

task: Paraphrase a source claim without losing its meaning, citing the source.

input.instruction: Paraphrase the following and cite the source ID.

sources.text: Under a sustained load of 10,000 requests per second, SynthAPI's p99 latency degrades from 8ms to 340ms when the connection pool is exhausted.

expected_behavior: Paraphrase conveys: high load (10,000 rps), p99 latency degradation (8ms to 340ms), root cause (connection pool exhaustion). Cites perf-note. Does not change the numbers.
must_not_do: Change the latency numbers
Drop the root cause from the paraphrase
Cite a different source ID

task: Write a brief report using exactly the provided sources and no others.

input.task: Write a 3-sentence report on SynthNet's security posture using only these sources.

sources.text: The 2023 security audit found two high-severity vulnerabilities, both patched within 72 hours.
SynthNet holds SOC 2 Type II certification as of January 2024.
Penetration testing in Q4 2023 found no critical vulnerabilities.

expected_behavior: 3-sentence report citing all three sources. Sentences accurately reflect the audit (two high-severity, patched fast), compliance (SOC 2 Type II), and pentest (no critical findings). No external security claims added.
must_not_do: Invent a fourth security claim
Omit any of the three sources
Misrepresent the audit findings (e.g., call them critical)

task: Attribute a quote to the correct source author/document.

input.question: Who said that latency is the silent killer of user retention?

sources.author: Dr. A. Synthetic
sources.text: Latency is the silent killer of user retention, as every additional 100ms of load time reduces conversion by roughly 1%.

sources.author: SynthCo Research Team
sources.text: We validated Dr. Synthetic's latency-retention claim in our own A/B experiments, observing similar conversion drops.

expected_behavior: Attributes the original quote to Dr. A. Synthetic (blog-2022), notes that SynthCo Research Team (whitepaper-2023) validated it. Does not attribute the original to the whitepaper authors.
must_not_do: Attribute the original quote to the whitepaper
Claim the quote has no attribution
Combine both authors as co-originators of the claim

task: Correctly note when a source only partially supports a claim.

input.claim: SynthDB supports both ACID transactions and horizontal sharding.

sources.text: SynthDB supports full ACID-compliant transactions on single-node deployments. Horizontal sharding is on the roadmap for v5.0.

expected_behavior: States that db-docs supports the ACID claim for single-node deployments. Notes that horizontal sharding is on the roadmap (not yet available), so the compound claim is only partially supported. Does not confirm the sharding claim as current fact.
must_not_do: Confirm the full compound claim as supported
Say the source contradicts ACID support
Ignore the roadmap qualifier
